Bash(python3 << 'PYEOF'
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

img = Image.open\('/Users/yutotakemori/Downloads/uruo.png'\).convert\('RGBA'\)
data = np.array\(img\)

# The background is white/near-white. Remove pixels where R,G,B are all > 240
r, g, b, a = data[:,:,0], data[:,:,1], data[:,:,2], data[:,:,3]

# White background removal: pixels with high brightness
brightness = r.astype\(float\) * 0.299 + g.astype\(float\) * 0.587 + b.astype\(float\) * 0.114

# Hard threshold for clearly white pixels
white_mask = \(r > 235\) & \(g > 235\) & \(b > 235\)
data[white_mask, 3] = 0

# Soft edge: near-white pixels get partial transparency  
near_white = \(brightness > 220\) & ~white_mask
# Calculate how white each pixel is \(0=not white, 1=fully white\)
whiteness = np.clip\(\(brightness - 200\) / 55, 0, 1\)
data[near_white, 3] = \(255 * \(1 - whiteness[near_white]\)\).astype\(np.uint8\)

# Also handle the shadow at the bottom \(grayish floor\)
# Light gray floor pixels
light_gray = \(brightness > 190\) & \(r > 180\) & \(g > 180\) & \(b > 180\) & ~white_mask & ~near_white
grayness = np.clip\(\(brightness - 170\) / 60, 0, 1\)
new_alpha = \(255 * \(1 - grayness * 0.7\)\).astype\(np.uint8\)
data[light_gray, 3] = np.minimum\(data[light_gray, 3], new_alpha[light_gray]\)

result = Image.fromarray\(data\)
result.save\('/Users/yutotakemori/new-game/smash-game/images/uruo.png'\)
print\(f"Done: {result.size}, saved to images/uruo.png"\)
PYEOF)

Bash(python3 << 'PYEOF'
from PIL import Image

img = Image.open\('/Users/yutotakemori/Downloads/uruo.png'\).convert\('RGBA'\)
pixels = img.load\(\)
w, h = img.size

for y in range\(h\):
    for x in range\(w\):
        r, g, b, a = pixels[x, y]
        brightness = r * 0.299 + g * 0.587 + b * 0.114
        
        # Fully white -> transparent
        if r > 235 and g > 235 and b > 235:
            pixels[x, y] = \(r, g, b, 0\)
        # Near-white -> partial transparency
        elif brightness > 215 and r > 200 and g > 200 and b > 200:
            whiteness = min\(max\(\(brightness - 200\) / 55, 0\), 1\)
            new_a = int\(255 * \(1 - whiteness\)\)
            pixels[x, y] = \(r, g, b, min\(a, new_a\)\)
        # Light gray floor shadow -> soften
        elif brightness > 185 and r > 170 and g > 170 and b > 170:
            grayness = min\(max\(\(brightness - 170\) / 60, 0\), 1\)
            new_a = int\(255 * \(1 - grayness * 0.6\)\)
            pixels[x, y] = \(r, g, b, min\(a, new_a\)\)

img.save\('/Users/yutotakemori/new-game/smash-game/images/uruo.png'\)
print\(f"Done: {w}x{h}"\)
PYEOF)

Bash(python3 << 'PYEOF'
from PIL import Image

img = Image.open\('/Users/yutotakemori/Downloads/uruo.png'\).convert\('RGBA'\)
pixels = img.load\(\)
w, h = img.size

# First pass: find the character bounding box by checking non-white areas
# The background is white \(>240 in all channels\)
# Use flood fill from edges to identify background

# Create a mask: True = background candidate
mask = [[False]*w for _ in range\(h\)]

# Mark all white-ish pixels
for y in range\(h\):
    for x in range\(w\):
        r, g, b, a = pixels[x, y]
        if r > 230 and g > 230 and b > 230:
            mask[y][x] = True

# Flood fill from all edges to find connected white background
from collections import deque
visited = [[False]*w for _ in range\(h\)]
queue = deque\(\)

# Add edge pixels that are white
for x in range\(w\):
    if mask[0][x] and not visited[0][x]:
        queue.append\(\(x, 0\)\)
        visited[0][x] = True
    if mask[h-1][x] and not visited[h-1][x]:
        queue.append\(\(x, h-1\)\)
        visited[h-1][x] = True
for y in range\(h\):
    if mask[y][0] and not visited[y][0]:
        queue.append\(\(0, y\)\)
        visited[y][0] = True
    if mask[y][w-1] and not visited[y][w-1]:
        queue.append\(\(w-1, y\)\)
        visited[y][w-1] = True

# BFS flood fill
while queue:
    cx, cy = queue.popleft\(\)
    for dx, dy in [\(-1,0\),\(1,0\),\(0,-1\),\(0,1\)]:
        nx, ny = cx+dx, cy+dy
        if 0 <= nx < w and 0 <= ny < h and not visited[ny][nx]:
            r, g, b, a = pixels[nx, ny]
            brightness = r * 0.299 + g * 0.587 + b * 0.114
            # More aggressive: include light pixels connected to background
            if r > 210 and g > 210 and b > 210:
                visited[ny][nx] = True
                queue.append\(\(nx, ny\)\)

# Apply: make background transparent
for y in range\(h\):
    for x in range\(w\):
        if visited[y][x]:
            r, g, b, a = pixels[x, y]
            pixels[x, y] = \(r, g, b, 0\)
        else:
            # Soft edge: if adjacent to background, partial alpha
            r, g, b, a = pixels[x, y]
            if r > 220 and g > 220 and b > 220:
                # Check if near background
                near_bg = False
                for dx, dy in [\(-1,0\),\(1,0\),\(0,-1\),\(0,1\),\(-2,0\),\(2,0\),\(0,-2\),\(0,2\)]:
                    nx, ny = x+dx, y+dy
                    if 0 <= nx < w and 0 <= ny < h and visited[ny][nx]:
                        near_bg = True
                        break
                if near_bg:
                    brightness = r * 0.299 + g * 0.587 + b * 0.114
                    fade = min\(max\(\(brightness - 200\) / 55, 0\), 1\)
                    pixels[x, y] = \(r, g, b, int\(255 * \(1 - fade\)\)\)

img.save\('/Users/yutotakemori/new-game/smash-game/images/uruo.png'\)
print\(f"Done: {w}x{h}"\)
PYEOF)

Bash(python3 << 'PYEOF'
from PIL import Image
from collections import deque

img = Image.open\('/Users/yutotakemori/new-game/smash-game/images/bullet.png'\).convert\('RGBA'\)
pixels = img.load\(\)
w, h = img.size
print\(f"Size: {w}x{h}"\)

# Sample edge pixels to identify the checkerboard colors
edge_colors = []
for x in range\(0, w, 5\):
    edge_colors.append\(pixels[x, 0][:3]\)
    edge_colors.append\(pixels[x, h-1][:3]\)
for y in range\(0, h, 5\):
    edge_colors.append\(pixels[0, y][:3]\)
    edge_colors.append\(pixels[w-1, y][:3]\)

# Count most common edge colors
from collections import Counter
color_counts = Counter\(edge_colors\)
print\("Top edge colors:", color_counts.most_common\(5\)\)

PYEOF)
